witnesses.description: The 3-4-5 right triangle has squared side lengths 9, 16, and 25.
Translation by (1,-2) preserves the triangle distance table.
Two fixed triangles share the same squared distance table.

checks.claim: The listed coordinates produce the listed triangle squared-distance table.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes all three squared distances over exact rationals.

checks.claim: The listed translation preserves the triangle squared-distance table.
checks.notes: The validator checks every target point equals source point plus the translation and that both distance tables match.

checks.claim: Translation by (1,-2) sends the source point (3,0) to a target with x-coordinate 5.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact translation replay computes target point (4,-2), while the malformed row claims x-coordinate 5; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates the final coordinate equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: The listed triangles have the same squared-distance table.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes both nondegenerate triangle distance tables and compares them.

checks.claim: The segment from (0,0) to (3,0) has squared distance 10.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact distance replay computes 9, while the malformed distance table claims 10; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: General rigidity, rigid-motion classification, and synthetic rigidity theorems require future Lean resources.

data.target_theorem_shape: graph rigidity, rigid-motion classification, and synthetic rigidity theorems over general point configurations
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies

checks.notes: The finite rows are checked by exact rational distance-table replay; general rigidity theory remains a proof-assistant horizon.
